No Poverty.reason: The mention of '部落創生' (tribal revitalization) implies efforts towards economic development, which can indirectly support Target 1.4 (equal rights to economic resources) for indigenous communities.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit evidence in the course description or objectives relates to food security, nutrition, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit evidence in the course description or objectives relates to health, well-being, disease prevention, or healthcare access.

Quality Education.reason: The course is explicitly 'Indigenous Education,' directly addressing equal access for indigenous peoples (Target 4.5) and teacher training (Target 4.c), which are core to ensuring quality education for all (Target 4.1).

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit evidence in the course description or objectives relates to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit evidence in the course description or objectives relates to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit evidence in the course description or objectives relates to energy access or renewable energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course's focus on '師資培育' (teacher training) and '原住民籍教師聘用與培育' (hiring and training of indigenous teachers) directly relates to creating decent employment opportunities (Target 8.5).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: No explicit evidence in the course description or objectives relates to industry, innovation, or infrastructure development.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course's core subject, 'Indigenous Education,' and its analysis of the 'Indigenous Peoples Education Act' directly address reducing inequalities for indigenous peoples (Target 10.2).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course's focus on '部落學校與部落創生' (tribal schools and revitalization) and '閱讀原住民族的文化繪本' (reading indigenous cultural picture books) links to safeguarding cultural heritage (Target 11.4) and strengthening communities.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit evidence in the course description or objectives relates to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or aquatic resources.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit evidence in the course description or objectives relates to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land management.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's analysis of the 'Indigenous Peoples Education Act' and '民族教育政策與法規' (ethnic education policy and regulations) directly addresses non-discriminatory laws (Target 16.b) and rule of law (Target 16.3).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Planned lectures by a '立法委員' (legislator) and a '校長' (school principal) demonstrate multi-stakeholder partnerships (Target 17.17) for indigenous education.
